mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand and construction activity, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with INDPRO acceleration during recovery phases with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 270 trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >65% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 270.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive 20-day returns in >55% of occurrences over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth against 60-day forward returns remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production signal manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances following two consecutive monthly INDPRO declines.
